title: Practitioner's Playbook - AI Ethics Compliance Checklist
subject: Do this today: The 20-minute AI ethics audit your firm needs before someone asks
preheader: A printable checklist for every practice area. Don't wait for a bar complaint.
bodyHtml:
LegalTech AI Hub

The Practitioner’s Playbook

Tactical guides you can use this week

The 20-Minute AI Ethics Audit for Your Firm

TIME: 20 MIN · DIFFICULTY: BEGINNER · IMPORTANCE: CRITICAL

With malpractice carriers adding AI exclusions and bar associations issuing ethics opinions, the window for “we’ll figure out our AI policy later” has closed. This guide gives you a concrete checklist to audit your firm’s AI practices today. Print it, work through it, and keep it on file. When someone asks — and they will — you’ll be ready.

Section 1: Data Security (5 minutes)

☐ List every AI tool your firm uses (include ChatGPT, Claude, Copilot, and any browser extensions)
☐ For each tool: Does client data leave your network? Where is it processed?
☐ For each tool: Review the privacy policy and terms of service. Does the vendor train on your inputs?
☐ Do you have a data processing agreement (DPA) with each AI vendor?
☐ Are any AI tools running on personal devices without firm oversight?

Section 2: Verification Workflows (5 minutes)

☐ Does your firm have a documented process for verifying AI-generated research?
☐ Are all AI-generated citations verified against primary sources before filing?
☐ Is there a human review step before any AI-generated content reaches a client?
☐ Do you maintain records showing human verification of AI outputs? (Critical for malpractice coverage)
☐ Is there a designated person responsible for AI quality assurance?

Section 3: Ethical Compliance (5 minutes)

☐ Do you know your jurisdiction’s current rules on AI disclosure in court filings?
☐ Does your engagement letter address AI use? (Increasingly expected by clients)
☐ Do you have a policy on which tasks AI may and may not be used for?
☐ Are all attorneys and staff trained on the firm’s AI use policy?
☐ Have you reviewed ABA Formal Opinion 523 and your state bar’s AI guidance?

Section 4: Insurance and Liability (5 minutes)

☐ Have you checked your malpractice policy for AI-related exclusions?
☐ Have you notified your carrier that the firm uses AI tools?
☐ Do you maintain documentation of your AI verification process? (Carriers are starting to require this)
☐ Do your vendor contracts include indemnification for AI errors?
☐ Is there a process for reporting and documenting AI errors when they occur?

SCORING

16-20 checked: Your firm is ahead of the curve. Keep documenting.
10-15 checked: Good foundation, but gaps that need attention this month.
5-9 checked: Material risk exposure. Prioritize this immediately.
Under 5: Stop reading this email and start the audit now. Seriously.

The firms that complete this audit now will be the ones that sleep well when the first AI-related bar complaint in their jurisdiction drops. And it will drop. The only question is whether your firm is the subject or the observer.

Need more detail on any of these areas? Our guides section has deep dives on AI ethics, vendor assessment, and building verification workflows.

Assess your overall readiness: take the Legal AI Readiness Quiz.
